headline: Server-authoritative expiry, one-time enrollment-secret destruction, and a vendored-framework security refresh
summary: A security-hardening release. Bundle retention becomes server-authoritative and individually-expired files stop being served or listed anywhere; one-time enrollment secrets are destroyed the moment they are delivered; chunked uploads gain an always-on scratch ceiling; and the vendored cryptography and identity framework is refreshed to its current release, bringing a broad batch of upstream fail-closed and injection hardening.

items.title: Bundle retention is server-authoritative
items.body: A bundle's expiry is now clamped to the operator's configured maximum. An uploader — including an anonymous one on the public drop page — can shorten a bundle's retention but can no longer request a window longer than the server allows, and every file in a bundle now expires together regardless of whether it arrived in a single request or in chunks. Previously a client could set an arbitrarily long retention, and a bundle's single-shot and chunked files could expire on different dates.

items.title: Individually-expired files are no longer served or listed
items.body: A file whose own expiry has passed is now withheld from the bundle browse page, the bundle JSON listing, the share page, and both the whole-bundle and per-folder ZIP downloads — matching the refusal the single-file download already returned. Previously such a file's bytes (through the ZIP paths) and its metadata (name, size, uploader) stayed reachable until the hourly sweep removed the record.

items.title: Enrollment provisioning secrets are destroyed at redemption
items.body: When a sync enrollment code is redeemed, its provisioning bundle — API key, client certificate and private key, and CA certificate — is now removed from the database the moment it is delivered to the client, rather than remaining on the redeemed row until the next hourly cleanup. The code stays single-use.

items.title: Chunked uploads bound their scratch space
items.body: Chunked upload now enforces an always-on per-bundle limit on staged (not-yet-assembled) chunk bytes, plus a per-file bound that applies even when an operator has lifted the file-size limit, so a stream of abandoned partial uploads can no longer exhaust the scratch disk. The abandoned-assembly cleanup now measures staleness from the last chunk actually written, so an active upload is never reclaimed mid-flight and a dormant one is reliably collected.

items.title: Empty values in encrypted columns carry tamper-evidence
items.body: An empty string stored in an encrypted database column is now sealed as an authenticated envelope rather than kept in the clear, so an attacker with database-write access cannot substitute a value for one.

items.title: Audit forwarding classifies events by exact action
items.body: When SIEM forwarding is enabled, each event's severity and outcome are derived from the exact action rather than a substring match. A refused or rate-limited event now surfaces as a warning a SIEM can alert on, and a deliberate administrative action such as lifting a suspension is no longer mislabeled as a failure.

items.title: Refreshed the vendored cryptography and identity framework
items.body: The bundled framework is updated to its current release, carrying a broad batch of upstream fail-closed and injection fixes across the cryptographic verifiers, the content and upload guards, the mail protocols, authentication, and request routing.

items.title: Sync file replacement preserves the prior file when it would exceed the size limit
items.body: Replacing a file in a sync bundle with a larger one that would push the bundle past its size limit now fails cleanly with the previous file intact. Previously the replacement deleted the old content and stored the new bytes before the limit was re-checked, so a rejected replacement reported failure while having already overwritten the file and left the bundle's size total understated.

items.title: Storage counters reflect reclaimed space after cleanup
items.body: Expired-file cleanup and empty-bundle removal now decrement the bundle and stash storage totals, so the admin dashboard's counts track space actually freed instead of drifting upward as files age out.

items.title: Bundle download count reflects real downloads
items.body: The whole-bundle ZIP download counter now increments only once at least one file has been packed, rather than on every request, so an aborted download or one where no file could be read no longer inflates the count.

items.title: Email template placeholders resolve declared values only
items.body: A template placeholder now resolves only an explicitly supplied value; a placeholder that names a built-in object property is left as literal text instead of rendering that property's contents.
